title: melaleuca quadrifida subsp. angustifolia
intro: Melaleuca quadrifida subsp. angustifolia, also known as Narrow-leaved Melaleuca or Narrow-leaved Paperbark, is a subspecies of plant in the myrtle family that is native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its essential oil. The health benefits of Melaleuca quadrifida subsp. angustifolia include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and combat infections. It can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or salve, and is often used topically to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
